title: LG Display Unveils a Host of Next-Generation Displays in the US - 동아사이언스

title: Samsung Electronics Announces New Leadership at the Visual Display Business
content: Samsung Electronics today announced the appointment of President Won-Jin Lee as the new Head of the Visual Display Business. President Seok Woo Yong, who has led the business, will serve as advisor to the Head of Device eXperience (DX) Division. President Won-Jin Lee, previously Head of Global Marketing Office, is an expert in contents, service […]

title: ADT lands US AI chip deal using Samsung 4nm
content: ADTechnology said it has signed a KRW40 billion (US$27.1 million) turnkey contract with a US-based AI fabless company to develop and supply HPC SoC chiplets for AI data-center applications using Samsung Foundry's 4nm process.

title: Huawei targets US$12 billion in AI chip sales as China firms seek Nvidia alternatives
content: Huawei is set to capture the largest share of China's AI chip market this year, with revenue expected to rise at least 60% as Chinese technology companies accelerate orders for domestic alternatives to Nvidia, according to the Financial Times.

title: Taiwanese Micro LED suppliers race to tap optical links for AI data centers
content: Micro LED is emerging as a potential contender in AI optical communications as the industry shift toward "optical replacing copper" gathers pace.

title: Samsung reshuffles TV leadership amid Chinese rivalry and service pivot
content: Samsung Electronics has replaced the head of its Visual Display (VD) business in a rare mid-cycle reshuffle, responding to weakening demand and intensifying global competition in the television market.

title: Apple's cash pivot: A new CEO and strategy for the AI arms race
content: In a landmark shift coinciding with its second quarter of fiscal 2026 results, Apple has announced that it will no longer maintain its formal "net cash neutral" target. This financial pivot comes as the company prepares for a significant leadership transition: John Ternus, the current hardware engineering chief, will succeed Tim Cook as CEO on September 1, 2026.

title: Samsung pulls ahead of SK Hynix as commodity DRAM prices surge
content: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are both riding a historic memory upcycle, but a profit gap of about KRW15 trillion (approx. US$10 billion) has opened between the two Korean chipmakers, driven largely by commodity DRAM rather than high-bandwidth memory (HBM), according to Sedaily.

title: Samsung foundry rebound gains steam as 4nm yield reportedly tops 80%
content: Samsung Electronics' foundry business is drawing renewed attention as its 4nm process reportedly reaches higher utilization, supported by HBM4 base-die demand and orders from global technology companies.
